packages.name: cheerio
packages.synopsis: Package cheerio is a dependency-free, standard-library-only HTML parsing and jQuery-style traversal library for Go, inspired by the Node.js "cheerio" package.
packages.doc:
Package cheerio is a dependency-free, standard-library-only HTML parsing and jQuery-style traversal library for Go, inspired by the Node.js "cheerio" package. It provides three cooperating layers, all implemented from scratch without golang.org/x/net or any other third-party code: - a hand-written HTML tokenizer and tree-construction parser; - a CSS selector engine covering the selectors used in everyday scraping; - a chainable, jQuery-style Selection API for querying and lightly mutating the resulting DOM. # Parsing Load parses an HTML string into a Document. Parsing is tolerant: void elements (br, img, hr, input, meta, link, ...) never receive children, self-closing syntax is honored, comments and doctypes are preserved, and unclosed or improperly nested tags are recovered using implicit-close rules similar to a browser (for example an open

is closed when a block element or a new

begins, and

  • , , and friends close open siblings). Raw-text elements (script, style, textarea, title) capture their content verbatim. HTML character references — named (&), decimal (&) and hexadecimal (&) — are decoded in text and attribute values. The parse tree is made of Node values. Each Node has a Type (DocumentNode, ElementNode, TextNode, CommentNode or DoctypeNode), a Data field (the lower-cased tag name for elements, otherwise the raw content), an ordered slice of Attribute values, and Parent/Children links. # Selectors The selector engine supports: - type selectors (div), the universal selector (*), #id and .class; - namespace-qualified type selectors (svg|rect, *|title), matched on the local name; - attribute selectors [attr], [attr=v], [attr^=v], [attr$=v], [attr*=v], [attr~=v] and [attr|=v], with an optional case-insensitivity flag ([attr=v i]); - selector lists separated by commas; - combinators: descendant (space), child (>), adjacent sibling (+) and general sibling (~); - structural pseudo-classes :first-child, :last-child, :only-child, :first-of-type, :last-of-type, :only-of-type, :empty, :root, :parent, :nth-child(n), :nth-last-child(n), :nth-of-type(n) and :nth-last-of-type(n) (each with the an+b microsyntax plus the odd/even keywords); - functional pseudo-classes :not(selector), :has(selector) and :contains(text); - form and content pseudo-classes :input, :checked, :selected, :enabled, :disabled and :header. Compound selectors combine any of the above (for example "ul.menu > li:first-child a[href^=\"/\"]"). # Selection API Document.Find runs a selector over the whole document and returns a Selection: an ordered, de-duplicated set of matched nodes. Selections are chainable. Traversal: Find, Children, Contents, Parent, Parents, ParentsUntil, Closest, Siblings, Next, Prev, NextAll, PrevAll, NextUntil, PrevUntil, Filter, FilterFunc, Not, Is, Has, Add, AddSelection, AddNodes, AddBack, End, Eq, First, Last, Even, Odd, Slice, Index, IndexSelector, IndexOfNode, Each, Map, Get and Length. Accessors: Attr, AttrOr, Prop, Text, Html (inner HTML), OuterHtml, ToString, HasClass, Css, Data, DataOr, DataMap, Val and TagName. Manipulation: SetAttr, SetAttrs, RemoveAttr, AddClass, RemoveClass, ToggleClass, SetText, SetHtml, SetVal, SetProp, SetCss, SetCssMap, SetData, Append, Prepend, Before, After, AppendTo, PrependTo, InsertBefore, InsertAfter, ReplaceWith, Remove, Empty, Clone, Wrap, WrapAll, WrapInner and Unwrap. Each content method has string, node and Selection accepting forms. Forms: SerializeArray returns the successful form controls of a selection as name/value pairs, and Serialize encodes them as an application/x-www-form-urlencoded string. # Example doc := cheerio.Load(`
    • One
    • Two
    `) doc.Find("li.a").Each(func(i int, n *cheerio.Node) { // ... }) fmt.Println(doc.Find("li").First().Text()) // One The library is deterministic: identical input always yields identical output, and Selections preserve document order.

types.name: Attribute
types.doc:
Attribute is a single HTML attribute. Attributes preserve their source
order within an element.

types.name: Document
types.doc:
Document is a parsed HTML document. It is the entry point for querying and
wraps the synthetic root node produced by the parser.

funcs.name: Load
funcs.doc:
Load parses an HTML fragment or full document and returns a Document.
Parsing never fails: malformed input is recovered as a browser would.

methods.name: Find
methods.doc: Find returns all elements in the document matching the selector.

methods.name: FindMatcher
methods.doc:
FindMatcher returns all elements in the document that satisfy the compiled
matcher. It is the pre-compiled equivalent of Find.

methods.name: Html
methods.doc:
Html returns the serialized HTML of the whole document, including doctype,
comments and top-level elements, in document order. It mirrors the static
cheerio $.html() call on a loaded document.

methods.name: Render
methods.doc:
Render writes the document's serialized HTML (see Html) to w and returns any
error reported by w.

methods.name: Root
methods.doc:
Root returns a Selection containing the document root node's element
children (the top-level elements of the parsed input).

methods.name: Text
methods.doc:
Text returns the concatenated text content of the entire document, in
document order. It mirrors the static cheerio $.text() call.

types.name: FormField
types.doc:
FormField is a single successful form control name/value pair produced by
SerializeArray.

types.name: Matcher
types.doc:
Matcher is a compiled CSS selector. Compiling a selector once and reusing the
resulting Matcher across many nodes or documents avoids the cost of parsing
the selector string on every call, which matters in tight scraping loops.

A Matcher is immutable after construction and safe for concurrent use by
multiple goroutines.

funcs.name: Compile
funcs.doc:
Compile parses selector into a reusable Matcher. It returns an error if
selector is empty or cannot be parsed. Unlike the string-accepting methods
(which silently treat an unparseable selector as matching nothing), Compile
reports malformed selectors so they can be caught at startup.

funcs.name: MustCompile
funcs.doc:
MustCompile is like Compile but panics if selector cannot be parsed. It is
intended for package-level Matcher variables initialized with known-good
selectors.

methods.name: Match
methods.doc: Match reports whether n satisfies the compiled selector.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns the original selector text the Matcher was compiled from.

types.name: Node
types.signature:
type Node struct {
	Type     NodeType
	Data     string      // tag name for elements, text content for text/comment/doctype
	Attr     []Attribute // ordered attributes (elements only)
	Parent   *Node
	Children []*Node
}
types.doc:
Node is a single node in the parsed DOM tree. Elements carry a lower-cased
Data (tag name) and an ordered slice of attributes; text, comment and
doctype nodes carry their content in Data.

funcs.name: Merge
funcs.doc:
Merge appends the nodes of tail to head, removing duplicates while preserving
first-seen order, and returns the combined slice. The input slices are not
modified. It mirrors the static cheerio.merge helper for combining node sets.

funcs.name: ParseHTML
funcs.doc:
ParseHTML parses an HTML fragment and returns its top-level nodes, detached
from any document root (each returned node has a nil Parent). It mirrors the
static cheerio.parseHTML helper and is convenient for building content to
hand to the manipulation methods (AppendNodes, BeforeNodes, ...).

Parsing never fails: malformed input is recovered the way a browser would.

methods.name: AppendChild
methods.doc: AppendChild adds c as the last child of n and sets c's parent.

methods.name: AttrValue
methods.doc:
AttrValue returns the value of the named attribute and whether it exists.
Attribute names are matched case-insensitively.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc:
Clone returns a deep copy of n. The copy has a nil Parent and fully
independent Children and Attr slices, so mutating either tree leaves the
other unchanged.

methods.name: FirstChild
methods.doc:
FirstChild returns the first child of n (of any node type), or nil if n has
no children.

methods.name: HasClass
methods.doc:
HasClass reports whether n is an element carrying the given class token in
its class attribute.

methods.name: InnerHTML
methods.doc:
InnerHTML returns the serialized HTML of n's children (everything inside the
element, excluding its own start and end tags). It corresponds to the DOM
innerHTML property and to Selection.Html.

methods.name: LastChild
methods.doc:
LastChild returns the last child of n (of any node type), or nil if n has no
children.

methods.name: Matches
methods.doc:
Matches reports whether n satisfies the given CSS selector. It compiles the
selector on each call; use Compile with Matcher.Match to reuse a selector
across many nodes.

methods.name: NextElementSibling
methods.doc:
NextElementSibling returns the nearest following sibling of n that is an
element, skipping text and comment nodes, or nil.

methods.name: NextSibling
methods.doc:
NextSibling returns the sibling immediately following n in its parent's child
list (of any node type, including text and comment nodes), or nil.

methods.name: OuterHTML
methods.doc:
OuterHTML returns the serialized HTML of n including its own tags. It
corresponds to the DOM outerHTML property and to Selection.OuterHtml.

methods.name: PrevElementSibling
methods.doc:
PrevElementSibling returns the nearest preceding sibling of n that is an
element, skipping text and comment nodes, or nil.

methods.name: PrevSibling
methods.doc:
PrevSibling returns the sibling immediately preceding n in its parent's child
list (of any node type, including text and comment nodes), or nil.

methods.name: RemoveAttr
methods.doc:
RemoveAttr deletes the named attribute from n if present. The name is matched
case-insensitively.

methods.name: Render
methods.doc:
Render writes the serialized outer HTML of n to w. It returns any error
reported by w. This is the streaming form of OuterHTML, useful for writing a
subtree directly to an http.ResponseWriter or file.

methods.name: SetAttr
methods.doc:
SetAttr sets (or inserts) the named attribute on n, preserving the source
order of existing attributes. The name is lower-cased. It has no effect on
non-element nodes.

methods.name: TagName
methods.doc:
TagName returns the lower-cased tag name of n if it is an element, otherwise
the empty string.

methods.name: Text
methods.doc:
Text returns the concatenated character data of n and all of its
descendants, in document order. It is the node-level counterpart of
Selection.Text and mirrors the DOM textContent property.

types.name: NodeType
types.doc: NodeType identifies the kind of a Node in the DOM tree.

types.name: Selection
types.doc:
Selection is a set of matched DOM nodes with jQuery-style traversal and
accessor methods. Selections are immutable with respect to their node set:
traversal methods return new Selections. The order of nodes is document
order and duplicates are removed.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc:
Add returns a new Selection combining the receiver with nodes matching the
selector across the whole document.

methods.name: AddBack
methods.doc:
AddBack adds the previous set of matched elements (the Selection this one was
derived from) to the current set, optionally filtered by a selector. It is
the inverse companion to End: where End discards the current stage, AddBack
merges it with the prior one.

methods.name: AddClass
methods.doc: AddClass adds one or more space-separated classes to every selected element.

methods.name: AddNodes
methods.doc:
AddNodes returns a new Selection combining the receiver with the given nodes,
in document order with duplicates removed.

methods.name: AddSelection
methods.doc:
AddSelection returns a new Selection combining the receiver with the nodes of
other, in document order with duplicates removed.

methods.name: After
methods.doc:
After parses each HTML string and inserts the resulting nodes after every
selected node in the tree.

methods.name: AfterNodes
methods.doc: AfterNodes inserts the given nodes after every selected node.

methods.name: AfterSelection
methods.doc: AfterSelection inserts the nodes of other after every selected node.

methods.name: Append
methods.doc:
Append parses each HTML string and appends the resulting nodes to the end of
the children of every selected element.

methods.name: AppendNodes
methods.doc:
AppendNodes appends the given nodes to the end of the children of every
selected element.

methods.name: AppendSelection
methods.doc:
AppendSelection appends the nodes of other to the end of the children of
every selected element.

methods.name: AppendTo
methods.doc:
AppendTo appends the selected nodes to every element matching selector and
returns the receiver.

methods.name: Attr
methods.doc:
Attr returns the value of the named attribute of the first selected node and
whether it exists.

methods.name: AttrOr
methods.doc:
AttrOr returns the value of the named attribute of the first node, or def if
it is absent (or the selection is empty).

methods.name: Attrs
methods.doc:
Attrs returns every attribute of the first selected element as a map keyed by
attribute name. It returns an empty (non-nil) map when the selection is empty
or the first node is not an element. This is the no-argument form of jQuery's
.attr() that returns the whole attribute set.

methods.name: Before
methods.doc:
Before parses each HTML string and inserts the resulting nodes before every
selected node in the tree.

methods.name: BeforeNodes
methods.doc: BeforeNodes inserts the given nodes before every selected node.

methods.name: BeforeSelection
methods.doc: BeforeSelection inserts the nodes of other before every selected node.

methods.name: Children
methods.doc:
Children returns the immediate element children of the selected nodes,
optionally filtered by a selector.

methods.name: Clone
methods.doc:
Clone returns a new Selection containing deep copies of the selected nodes.
The clones are detached from the document.

methods.name: Closest
methods.doc:
Closest returns, for each selected node, the nearest ancestor-or-self that
matches the selector.

methods.name: Contents
methods.doc:
Contents returns the children of each selected node, including text and
comment nodes (not only elements). This mirrors jQuery's .contents().

methods.name: Css
methods.doc:
Css returns the value of the named inline CSS property of the first selected
element, or "" if the property (or the selection) is absent.

methods.name: Data
methods.doc:
Data returns the value of the data-* attribute for key on the first selected
element and whether it exists. The key may be given in camelCase ("fooBar")
or dashed ("foo-bar") form; both map to the data-foo-bar attribute.

methods.name: DataMap
methods.doc:
DataMap returns all data-* attributes of the first selected element as a map
keyed by camelCase name (data-foo-bar becomes "fooBar"). It returns an empty
map when the selection is empty or has no data attributes.

methods.name: DataOr
methods.doc:
DataOr returns the data-* value for key on the first selected element, or def
if it is absent.

methods.name: Each
methods.doc:
Each invokes fn for every selected node in order and returns the receiver
for chaining.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc:
Empty removes all child nodes from every selected element and returns the
receiver.

methods.name: End
methods.doc:
End returns the Selection that this one was derived from, restoring the
previous stage of a traversal chain (jQuery's .end()). If the receiver was
not derived from another Selection (for example a top-level Document.Find),
End returns the document root selection.

methods.name: Eq
methods.doc:
Eq returns a Selection containing only the node at index i (negative counts
from the end). An out-of-range index yields an empty Selection.

methods.name: Even
methods.doc: Even returns the even-indexed nodes of the selection (0, 2, 4, ...).

methods.name: Filter
methods.doc: Filter returns the subset of selected nodes that match the selector.

methods.name: FilterFunc
methods.signature: func (s *Selection) FilterFunc(pred func(i int, n *Node) bool) *Selection
methods.doc:
FilterFunc returns the subset for which the predicate returns true. The
index is the position within the current selection.

methods.name: FilterMatcher
methods.doc:
FilterMatcher returns the subset of selected nodes that satisfy the compiled
matcher. It is the pre-compiled equivalent of Filter.

methods.name: Find
methods.doc:
Find returns the descendants of the selected nodes that match the selector.

A selector member that begins with a combinator is treated as relative to
each selected node (its :scope): "> li" matches immediate element children,
"+ .b" the immediately following sibling and "~ .b" any following sibling.
Ordinary members continue to match descendants only.

methods.name: FindMatcher
methods.doc:
FindMatcher returns the descendants of the selected nodes that satisfy the
compiled matcher. It is the pre-compiled equivalent of Find.

methods.name: First
methods.doc: First returns the first node as a Selection.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc: Get returns the node at index i (negative counts from the end), or nil.

methods.name: Has
methods.doc: Has returns the selected nodes that have a descendant matching the selector.

methods.name: HasClass
methods.doc: HasClass reports whether any selected element carries the given class.

methods.name: Html
methods.doc:
Html returns the inner HTML of the first selected node (its serialized
children).

methods.name: Index
methods.doc:
Index returns the position of the first selected node among its element
siblings (0-based), or -1 if the selection is empty. It is the zero-argument
form of jQuery's .index().

methods.name: IndexOfNode
methods.doc:
IndexOfNode returns the 0-based position of node within the current
selection, or -1 if it is not present. It corresponds to jQuery's
.index(element).

methods.name: IndexSelector
methods.doc:
IndexSelector returns the 0-based position of the first selected node within
the set of elements matching selector across the document, or -1 if it is not
found. It corresponds to jQuery's .index(selector).

methods.name: InsertAfter
methods.doc:
InsertAfter inserts the selected nodes after every element matching selector
and returns the receiver.

methods.name: InsertBefore
methods.doc:
InsertBefore inserts the selected nodes before every element matching
selector and returns the receiver.

methods.name: Is
methods.doc: Is reports whether at least one selected node matches the selector.

methods.name: IsMatcher
methods.doc:
IsMatcher reports whether at least one selected node satisfies the compiled
matcher. It is the pre-compiled equivalent of Is.

methods.name: Last
methods.doc: Last returns the last node as a Selection.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns the number of nodes in the selection.

methods.name: Map
methods.signature: func (s *Selection) Map(fn func(i int, n *Node) string) []string
methods.doc: Map applies fn to each node and returns the collected results.

methods.name: MapNodes
methods.doc:
MapNodes applies fn to each selected node in order and returns the collected
non-nil results as a slice of nodes. It is the node-returning companion of
Map (which collects strings), matching jQuery's .map() when the callback
yields elements.

methods.name: Next
methods.doc: Next returns the immediately following element sibling of each node.

methods.name: NextAll
methods.doc: NextAll returns all following element siblings of each node.

methods.name: NextUntil
methods.doc:
NextUntil returns the following element siblings of each selected node, up to
(but not including) the first sibling that matches selector. An optional
second selector filters the collected elements.

methods.name: Nodes
methods.doc: Nodes returns the underlying nodes. Callers must not mutate the slice.

methods.name: Not
methods.doc: Not returns the selected nodes that do NOT match the selector.

methods.name: Odd
methods.doc: Odd returns the odd-indexed nodes of the selection (1, 3, 5, ...).

methods.name: OuterHtml
methods.doc:
OuterHtml returns the serialized HTML of the first selected node, including
the node itself.

methods.name: Parent
methods.doc: Parent returns the direct parent of each selected node (elements only).

methods.name: Parents
methods.doc:
Parents returns all ancestors of each selected node, nearest first within
each node's chain, optionally filtered by a selector.

methods.name: ParentsUntil
methods.doc:
ParentsUntil returns the ancestors of each selected node, nearest first, up
to (but not including) the first ancestor that matches selector. An optional
second selector filters the collected elements.

methods.name: Prepend
methods.doc:
Prepend parses each HTML string and inserts the resulting nodes at the start
of the children of every selected element.

methods.name: PrependNodes
methods.doc:
PrependNodes inserts the given nodes at the start of the children of every
selected element.

methods.name: PrependSelection
methods.doc:
PrependSelection inserts the nodes of other at the start of the children of
every selected element.

methods.name: PrependTo
methods.doc:
PrependTo prepends the selected nodes to every element matching selector and
returns the receiver.

methods.name: Prev
methods.doc: Prev returns the immediately preceding element sibling of each node.

methods.name: PrevAll
methods.doc: PrevAll returns all preceding element siblings of each node.

methods.name: PrevUntil
methods.doc:
PrevUntil returns the preceding element siblings of each selected node, up to
(but not including) the first sibling that matches selector. An optional
second selector filters the collected elements.

methods.name: Prop
methods.doc:
Prop returns a property of the first selected element and whether it is
defined. Boolean properties (checked, selected, disabled, ...) return "true"
or "false" based on the presence of the corresponding attribute. The special
properties "tagName"/"nodeName" return the upper-cased tag; "outerHTML",
"innerHTML" and "textContent" return the serialized or text content. Any
other name falls back to the attribute of the same name.

methods.name: Remove
methods.doc:
Remove detaches every selected node (optionally filtered by selector) from
the document and returns the receiver.

methods.name: RemoveAttr
methods.doc: RemoveAttr removes an attribute from every selected element.

methods.name: RemoveClass
methods.doc:
RemoveClass removes one or more space-separated classes from every selected
element. Removing all classes leaves an empty class attribute.

methods.name: RemoveData
methods.doc:
RemoveData removes data-* attributes from every selected element and returns
the receiver. With no arguments it removes all data-* attributes; otherwise
only the named keys are removed. Keys may be given in camelCase ("fooBar") or
dashed ("foo-bar") form.

methods.name: Render
methods.doc:
Render writes the serialized outer HTML of every selected node, in document
order, to w. It returns any error reported by w. Render is the streaming form
of ToString, avoiding an intermediate string when writing to an
http.ResponseWriter, a file, or any other io.Writer.

methods.name: ReplaceWith
methods.doc:
ReplaceWith replaces every selected node with the nodes parsed from html and
returns the receiver (whose nodes are now detached).

methods.name: ReplaceWithNodes
methods.doc:
ReplaceWithNodes replaces every selected node with the given nodes. For every
target but the last the replacement nodes are cloned.

methods.name: ReplaceWithSelection
methods.doc: ReplaceWithSelection replaces every selected node with the nodes of other.

methods.name: Serialize
methods.doc:
Serialize returns the selection's successful form controls encoded as an
application/x-www-form-urlencoded string (e.g. "a=1&b=2"), matching jQuery's
.serialize().

methods.name: SerializeArray
SetAttr
methods.doc: SetAttr sets an attribute on every selected element and returns the receiver.

methods.name: SetAttrs
methods.doc:
SetAttrs sets every key/value pair of attrs on all selected elements and
returns the receiver.

methods.name: SetCss
methods.doc:
SetCss sets a single inline CSS property on every selected element and
returns the receiver. Setting an empty value removes the property.

methods.name: SetCssMap
methods.doc:
SetCssMap sets every property/value pair of styles on all selected elements
and returns the receiver. Because Go maps have no order, callers that require
a specific declaration order should use SetCss for each property.

methods.name: SetData
methods.doc:
SetData sets the data-* attribute for key on every selected element and
returns the receiver. The key is normalized to dashed form.

methods.name: SetHtml
methods.doc:
SetHtml parses html and replaces the children of every selected element with
the resulting nodes. For every target but the first the nodes are cloned.

methods.name: SetProp
methods.doc:
SetProp sets a property on every selected element and returns the receiver.
For boolean properties a value of "" or "false" removes the attribute and any
other value adds it; other properties set the attribute of the same name.

methods.name: SetText
methods.doc:
SetText replaces the children of every selected element with a single text
node.

methods.name: SetVal
methods.doc:
SetVal sets the value of every selected form control and returns the
receiver. For textarea elements the value replaces the text content; for a
select the matching option is marked selected (others deselected); for other
controls the "value" attribute is set.

methods.name: Siblings
methods.doc:
Siblings returns the element siblings of each selected node (excluding the
node itself), optionally filtered by a selector.

methods.name: Slice
methods.doc:
Slice returns the nodes in [start, end) as a Selection. Negative indices
count from the end; out-of-range bounds are clamped.

methods.name: TagName
methods.doc: TagName returns the tag name of the first selected element, or "".

methods.name: Text
methods.doc:
Text returns the concatenated text content of every selected node and its
descendants, in document order.

methods.name: ToArray
methods.doc:
ToArray returns a copy of the selected nodes as a slice. Unlike Nodes, the
returned slice is safe to mutate; it mirrors jQuery's .toArray().

methods.name: ToString
methods.doc:
ToString returns the serialized outer HTML of every selected node,
concatenated in document order.

methods.name: ToggleClass
methods.doc: ToggleClass toggles each space-separated class on every selected element.

methods.name: Unwrap
methods.doc:
Unwrap removes the parent of every selected node, promoting the node (and its
siblings) into the grandparent. Each distinct parent is unwrapped once.

methods.name: Val
methods.doc:
Val returns the value of a form control: the "value" attribute for inputs,
the selected option for selects, or the text content for textareas.

methods.name: Wrap
methods.doc:
Wrap wraps every selected node in a clone of the structure described by html.
Each node is placed inside the innermost descendant of its wrapper.

methods.name: WrapAll
methods.doc:
WrapAll wraps all selected nodes together in a single structure described by
html, placed at the position of the first node.

methods.name: WrapInner
methods.doc:
WrapInner wraps the contents (child nodes) of every selected element in a
clone of the structure described by html.

funcs.name: Contains
funcs.doc:
Contains reports whether contained is a descendant of container (the two must
be distinct nodes). It mirrors the static cheerio.contains helper and the DOM
Node.contains method restricted to strict descendants.

funcs.name: RenderNodes
funcs.doc:
RenderNodes returns the serialized outer HTML of every supplied node,
concatenated in argument order. It mirrors the static cheerio.html helper
applied to a set of nodes.

funcs.name: TextOf
funcs.doc:
TextOf returns the concatenated text content of every supplied node and its
descendants, in argument order. It mirrors the static cheerio.text helper.

examples.name: Selection (Append)
Selection (Map)
Selection (SerializeArray)
Selection (Wrap)
